blocks.content: He went to the park on a Tuesday morning with his supplies and the permission form he had spent three weeks obtaining.
It was the same park. The fountain still ran. The bench he had been sitting on the first time he saw her was still there — the same bench, or looked the same, the same particular corner of the world.
He sat on it for a few minutes before he began.

He thought about himself at nineteen, sitting here with a sketchbook, drawing the world the way he always had — with the patience of someone who understood that the thing worth noticing was usually the thing most people walked past.
He thought about the moment she turned around.
